title: oxalis leucolepis var. griffithii
intro: Oxalis leucolepis var. griffithii, commonly known as the pink sorrel or Indian sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oxalis leucolepis var. griffithii include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to make a juice or paste that is believed to have various health benefits.
